dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6924 IH: Porch Pirates Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Gottheimer (for himself, Mr. Van Drew, Ms. Van Duyne, Mr. Moskowitz, Mr. Peters, Mr. Rutherford, Mrs. Kim, Ms. Tenney, Mr. Pappas, and Mr. Panetta) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend title 18, United States Code, to extend to matter left for collection by private or commercial interstate carriers the same protections as matter carried throughout the country as interstate or foreign commerce.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Porch Pirates Act of 2025.

It is the sense of Congress that Congress has the authority to extend to matters left for collection by private or commercial interstate carriers the same protections as matter carried through the country as interstate or foreign commerce.

section.header: Private or commercial interstate carrier protections

subsection.header: Obstruction of matter left for collection
subsection.text: Section 659 of title 18, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: at the end of the fourth paragraph, by striking — and inserting ; or; and
by inserting after paragraph four the following new paragraph:

quoted-block.text: Whoever embezzles, steals, or unlawfully takes, carries away, or by fraud or deception obtains, any package, or other article, which has been delivered by any private or commercial interstate carrier before the addressee or his or her agent has taken physical possession—
